prep.summary: Review your team's weekly activity—it highlights severe capacity constraints on the architecture team due to recent high-profile departures—while strategic transformation pushes forward in underwriting and the Life Company. Manage your technical execution alongside a major cybersecurity uplift program—this hygiene initiative alone threatens a 15–20% capacity hit on current delivery schedules. Push for consolidated business cases on the Skan.ai RFP while navigating the compliance hurdles halting your claims evolution tools.
tasks: Engage with Deepak to define clear decision-making authority—resolving the leadership and accountability gaps in the P&C architecture space.
Consolidate the scattered business cases from Claims, DNS, and Agency—building a unified P&C view to support the upcoming Skan.ai RFP pilot.
Realign delivery schedules immediately—adjusting project timelines to absorb the 15–20% capacity loss driven by the cybersecurity uplift program.
Resolve the security and compliance blocks regarding PII/NPI sharing—clearing the path for the HiMarley visual voicemail integration.
prep.title: TF Standup

events.summary: GenAI Program & Use Case Demos: Open Forum

prep.summary: Your Google Drive contains no files with "Abe" in the title—nor do your recent notes mention Rebecca or Celonis directly. The Skan.ai RFP notes from June 11 cover process and task mining—topics highly relevant to Celonis capabilities. You must address these information gaps—especially regarding personnel challenges and organizational complexity—during your 1:1.
tasks: Locate the missing files containing "Abe" in the title—either by checking alternative storage folders or syncing with team members.
Investigate the discussions regarding Rebecca—specifically her current alignment with the underwriting or claims evolution initiatives.
Compare the Skan.ai task mining capabilities against Celonis—focusing on the target production pilot timeline for September.
Resolve the decision-making bottleneck in P&C architecture with Deepak—addressing the capacity constraints caused by recent leadership departures.
prep.title: Monthly 1:1w/ Abe Capetillo | Mark Francis
